'''David Allen''' (born 15 September 1985) is a former [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward. An [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international, Allen played for the [[Wigan Warriors]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], [[Barrow Raiders]], [[Widnes Vikings]], [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] and the [[Rochdale Hornets]].

==Club career==

Allen was included in the [[Wigan Warriors]] squad in 2003 and become a first team player in 2005. He played 25 matches for Wigan mostly as a substitute and was released at the end of the 2005's [[Super League X]].<ref name="WT14Oct05"/>

After being released from [[Wigan Warriors]] he went on to join [[RFL Championship|National League One]] side [[Widnes Vikings]].<ref name="WT14Oct05">{{cite web |title=Widnes snap up youngsters |url=https://www.wigantoday.net/news/widnes-snap-up-youngsters-1-164473 |website=Wigan Today |accessdate=2 July 2019 |date=14 October 2005 |quote=Two more Wigan players parted company with the club today. Dave Allen and Bob Beswick have joined Widnes Vikings... |archive-date=2 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190702225345/https://www.wigantoday.net/news/widnes-snap-up-youngsters-1-164473 |url-status=dead }}</ref> He was one of seven ex-Wigan players in the 2006 Widnes side and regularly played in the second row. He played in the 2006 [[RFL Championship|National League One]] Grand Final where Widnes lost 16–29 to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. At the end of 2006 his contract with Widnes was not renewed after he failed a drug test following the Grand Final game against the Hull KR.<ref name="Reu1Mar07">{{cite web |last1=Phillips |first1=Mitch |title=Allen's "accidental cocaine" ban doubled |url=https://uk.reuters.com/article/uk-rugby-league-cocaine/allens-accidental-cocaine-ban-doubled-idUKL0152565920070301 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190702230621/https://uk.reuters.com/article/uk-rugby-league-cocaine/allens-accidental-cocaine-ban-doubled-idUKL0152565920070301 |url-status=dead |archive-date=2 July 2019 |website=Reuters |accessdate=2 July 2019 |date=1 March 2007}}</ref><ref name="NWL8Mar07">{{cite web |title=Vikings star in drugs shame |url=https://www.dailypost.co.uk/news/local-news/vikings-star-in-drugs-shame-2877421 |website=North Wales Live |accessdate=2 July 2019 |date=8 March 2007}}</ref> Allen was banned from rugby league for 12 months by the [[Rugby Football League]] after testing positive for a metabolite of [[cocaine]], doubled to two years following an appeal by [[UK Sport]].<ref name="Reu1Mar07"/><ref name="NWL8Mar07"/>

After serving his ban, he joined [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] ahead of the 2009 season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Allen handed Oldham opportunity|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7728879.stm|work=BBC Sport|access-date=22 October 2013|date=14 November 2008}}</ref> After a brief spell with [[Barrow Raiders]], Allen joined Widnes for the second time in 2010 and was made club captain.<ref name="LE19May10">{{cite web |title=Widnes Vikings coach Paul Cullen makes Dave Allen new captain |url=https://www.liverpoolecho.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/widnes-vikings-coach-paul-cullen-3423571 |website=Liverpool Echo |accessdate=2 July 2019 |date=19 May 2010}}</ref><ref name="BBC18May10">{{cite web |title=Widnes Vikings forward Dave Allen given club captaincy |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8690210.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=2 July 2019 |date=18 May 2010}}</ref> He made 26 appearances in the 2012 season and, in October, signed a new contract until the end of the 2014 season.<ref name="BBC26Oct12">{{cite web |title=Dave Allen extends Widnes Vikings deal until 2014 |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/20103272 |website=BBC Sport |accessdate=2 July 2019 |date=26 October 2012}}</ref> He was released at the end of the 2014 season,<ref name="BBC10Sep14">{{cite web |title=Widnes Vikings to release Dave Allen and Kieran Butterworth |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/29150482 |website=BBC Sport |accessdate=2 July 2019 |date=10 September 2014}}</ref> and was signed by [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Whitehaven sign Widnes Vikings back-rower Dave Allen|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/30004258|website=BBC Sport|access-date=25 January 2015|date=11 November 2014}}</ref> Injury prevented him from playing during 2017 and he signed for [[Rochdale Hornets]] at the end of the season.<ref name="LRL3Oct17">{{cite web |last1=Gordon |first1=James |title=Allen plays on with Rochdale |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/allen-plays-rochdale/ |website=Love Rugby League |accessdate=2 July 2019 |date=3 October 2017}}</ref><ref name="RH1Oct17">{{cite web |title=New signing Allen looking to 'do something special' with Hornets |url=https://www.hornetsrugbyleague.co.uk/article/50273/new-signing-allen-looking-to-do-something-special-with-hornets |publisher=Rochdale Hornets |accessdate=2 July 2019 |date=1 October 2017}}</ref>

==International career==
He was included in the 2003 England Academy squad to face the [[Australian Institute of Sport]]. He was selected for the 2004 Academy Origin Series and the 2004 England Academy U-18s tour. In 2006 he was selected for Ireland along with six other Widnes players for the country's World Cup qualifying matches.

In 2016, he was called up to the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe#Group B|2017 Rugby League World Cup European Pool B qualifiers]].<ref name="Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14763089.Warrington_Wolves_trio_named_in_Ireland_squad_for_World_Cup_qualifiers|title=Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers|work=Warrington Guardian|date=24 September 2016|accessdate=26 September 2016}}</ref>

==Trivia==
* Allen's father, John, was a professional player with [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]].
* He attended Fairfield High School in [[Widnes]] from 1997 to 2002.
